exemplar.title: To what extent has the recent expansion of the MRT been effective in reducing the market failure associated with the overconsumption of private vehicles in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the recent expansion of the MRT been effective in reducing the market failure associated with the overconsumption of private vehicles in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic and economic context communicated accurately and efficiently
Research question stated verbatim, focused and maintained throughout
Methodology draws on multiple relevant secondary sources and a primary interview
weaknesses: Minor narrative repetition in rationale section creates inefficiency
Methodology lacks specific estimation techniques and full dataâcollection details
A Facebook URL undermines source credibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a highly effective focus and purpose, with a precise research question and wellâsuited scope. Two strands clearly meet the 5â6 band descriptors, while methodology falls slightly shorter due to omitted details, placing the overall performance at a 5.

strengths: Most sources are clearly relevant and well integrated into the discussion
Clear and coherent microÂ­economic knowledge applied to MRT case
Terminology (MSC, MPB, deadâweight loss) is used accurately and consistently
weaknesses: Some citations lack full bibliographic detail or direct relevance
Captions and examples occasionally refer to incorrect contexts (e.g., cigarettes)
Minor conceptual slips (merit good vs public good) reduce coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Application of source material, discipline knowledge and use of terminology consistently fall within the 3â4 band. While the work shows clear understanding and mostly appropriate application, occasional inaccuracies and irrelevant citations prevent a higher score.

strengths: Wide variety of appropriate, current sources tied directly to the research question
Logical argument structure linking theory, evidence and policy alternatives
Some critical evaluation of limitations and policy comparisons
weaknesses: Analysis depth is uneven and lacks rigorous quantification (e.g., per capita normalization)
Evaluation is only partially critical; costâbenefit and elasticity discussions are missing
Peripheral sources slightly weaken overall relevance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality sits in the upper half of band 4â6, with analysis and evaluation at the lower end. On balance, the student displays solid critical thinking but stops short of consistently rigorous, critical evaluationâjustifying a midâband score of 5.

strengths: Clearly structured report with standard EE conventions (TOC, headings, annexes)
Consistent layout, neat formatting, page numbers and figure captions present
Word count and title correctly displayed
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers for entries
Occasional broken URLs and redundant headings reduce navigational clarity
Some captions include overly detailed explanations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all the conventions of the top band but contains minor lapses (missing TOC page numbers, broken links, redundant headings), placing it solidly at a 3 rather than a perfect 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is quoted verbatim at the start and kept visible throughout the essay and in interview materials. It is concise, contains clear economic qualifiers (extent, negative externalities, MRT, Kuala Lumpur) and remains the organising thread for later analysis and conclusion sections. Minor breadth remains (health, emissions, accidents) preventing a razor-sharp quantitative focus, hence 5 rather than 6.

The student states the broad topic, geographical area and economic context immediately, then narrows these into a precise research focus on MRT expansion and market failure in Kuala Lumpur. The scope (three specific outcome variables) is explicit and well-suited to a systematic investigation. Purpose, relevance and economic rationale are therefore communicated accurately and efficiently, meeting the descriptors for the highest band. A perfect 6 is not awarded because some minor sections (e.g. âRationaleâ) verge on narrative repetition, showing small inefficiencies in communication.

Most data, quotations and visual material clearly relate to the MRT and vehicle over-consumption, and are woven into the text. The private-car registration series, emissions savings figures and interview excerpts are referenced and applied. Nevertheless, some citations (e.g., Poland travel-time study) add limited relevance, and key numerical comparisons (before/after ridership) are asserted rather than fully shown. Application is therefore âmostly relevantâ, consistent with band 3â4.

Standard EE structure (intro, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography, appendix) is present and logical, with headers and a table of contents. Some headings are redundant and pagination resets in excerpts, so minor weaknesses remain, warranting a solid but not perfect score in the top band.

Font, spacing, citation style and diagrams are neat; word count, title and sections are provided. The excerpt shows page numbers and figure captions, fulfilling IB layout conventions. Occasional broken URLs and a few long un-wrapped links show small lapses, so the strand earns 3 rather than 4.

Arguments follow a logical structure (theory â evidence â interim â comparison), showing some evaluation of policy alternatives (congestion charge) and limitations (coverage, weather). Evaluation is present but only partially critical; costâbenefit figures, elasticity or counter-evidence are missing. The discussion therefore fits the âreasoned argument with partially critical evaluationâ description.

The essay interprets diagrams, calculates emission savings and discusses behavioural factors, moving beyond description. However, depth is uneven: the link from emissions reduction figures to welfare gains is asserted, not analytically derived; vehicle-registration trend is described but not adjusted for population growth. Conclusions are âmostly supportedâ but lack rigorous quantification, situating analysis at the lower end of the 4â6 band.

The student demonstrates sound micro-economic knowledge: negative vs positive externalities, merit/demerit goods, welfare-loss triangles and congestion charging. Explanations are coherent and integrated into real-world context. Some slipsâequating MPB diagram labels to cigarettes, minor confusion over âmerit good vs public goodââprevent a fully âcoherentâ 5â6 score.

A wide set of sources (government statistics, peer-reviewed studies, interview, comparative Singapore case) is marshalled and clearly linked to the RQ. The majority are appropriate and current (post-2017 ridership, 2024 articles). Some peripheral sources (Monaco sustainability FAQ) weaken overall relevance slightly, aligning with the upper half of the 4â6 band.

The student identifies secondary statistical sources (DOSM, MOT, ridership data) and one primary interview, and explains why multiple sources are needed to mitigate bias. However, the plan for estimating air-quality changes is vague and no data-collection instruments are appended. Some key variables (vehicle kilometres, elasticity estimates) are absent. Thus the range is âgenerally relevantâ but evidence of *effective selection* is partial, placing the work in the 3â4 band.

Key terms (MSC, MPB, dead-weight loss, nudge theory) are used consistently and correctly, and diagrams are accurately labelled. Occasional inaccuraciesâcalling MRT a âsocial or public goodâ without distinction and a typo in âliabilityâ for âreliabilityââreduce precision. Overall terminology is âmostly accurateâ, meriting the 3â4 band.

annotations.comment: The title is prominently displayed and clearly indicates the investigationâs focus, fulfilling IB formatting guidelines.
The topic statement communicates the economic context and focus effectively, laying a strong foundation for systematic investigation.
Page numbering in the footer is consistent with IB requirements and aids reader orientation.
While the COâ emission figures are compelling, the citation ân.d.â lacks a publication year; ensure full bibliographic details.
The use of a Facebook URL undermines source credibility; replace with an official MRT Corp publication or government document.
The research purpose statement cuts off midâsentence; complete it and explicitly outline which variables will be analysed.
The rationale section relies heavily on historical narrative; strengthen it by explicitly linking the invention of cars to your RQ and economic models.
The footer spacing on this page varies from previous pages; standardize footers for a consistent professional look.
The hypothesis is wellâstated but could be sharpened by specifying which measurable indicators will confirm MRT effectiveness.
Correct the citation format â(Blink) et al., 2011/2020)â; verify author order and consistent year referencing.
The research question is concise, focused on MRT expansion and market failure, and remains visibly linked throughout the essay.
Inclusion of the word count at the start satisfies the formatting requirement and shows attention to IB conventions.
The table of contents is comprehensive but lacks page numbers for entries, reducing navigational clarity.
The âTable of Contentsâ header is clearly formatted and centres attention appropriately.
Ensure the 2024 sustainability management reference has full details (author, publisher) and explain its direct relevance to the MRT context.
The definition of private vehicles as demerit goods is correct; augment with welfare-loss triangle notation for precision.
Figure 1 illustrates overconsumption effectively, but the caption refers to cigarettes rather than vehicles; revise accordingly.
The 5-year rule justification is valid but reads narratively; link this discussion more directly to data availability and reliability.
The methodology mentions secondary data biases but omits precise estimation techniques (e.g., regression models, elasticity calculations).
The plan to estimate airâquality changes is vague; propose specific methodologies (e.g., differenceâinâdifferences with control cities) to enhance validity.
The footer alignment returns but still varies slightly; ensure uniform placement across all pages.
Conducting a primary interview enriches insight, but detail your protocol and coding process to address potential interviewer bias.
The âFindings and discussionâ heading is well-positioned, guiding the reader through the analytical progression.
The caption explanation is thorough but overly detailed; condense to the key relationships shown in the diagram.
Inline math variables (e.g., QT_{so}) are correct in content; ensure they are formatted in LaTeX ($$...$$) in your final version.
The RM6.8 billion fuelâcost statistic is impactful, but normalizing per capita or presenting margin of error would deepen your analysis.
Interview insights on positive externalities are valuable; triangulate with independent studies quantifying ridership benefits.
